theme: The office has furniture — now give it weather, memory, and gossip.

ideas.title: Conference Table Spontaneous Huddles
ideas.description: The conference table exists in CSS (.conference-table, .char--seated, .conf-meeting-badge) but idle workers never spontaneously gather there. When 2+ workers are idle simultaneously, occasionally animate them walking to the conference table seats for a 'sync' or 'coffee chat'. Show the conf-meeting-badge with playful labels like 'quick sync' or 'vibe check'. WHY: idle workers just wander aimlessly — the conference table is a perfect set piece that's going completely unused. Meetings are a universal office experience.

ideas.title: Activity Log Worker Emoji Avatars
ideas.description: The activity log shows .activity-worker-emoji but all entries look the same. Each worker has a unique emoji (Luna 🌙, Carlos ⚙️, Maya 🎨, Dave 🔧, Sofia 🔍). Show their emoji avatar inline before their name in every log entry. WHY: scanning a wall of text for who did what is tedious. A splash of emoji makes the log scannable at a glance — same trick Slack uses with profile photos.

ideas.title: Office Window Weather Sync
ideas.description: The office window (.office-window) shows a sky gradient with stars and clouds, but it's purely cosmetic — disconnected from any real data. Sync the window sky to Osvaldo's actual local weather via wttr.in: rain shows animated droplets on the glass, clouds drift across, clear sky shows sun/moon. WHY: this tiny detail would make people do a double-take — 'wait, is that MY weather?' It bridges the virtual office to the real world.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Task Queue Preview
ideas.description: The whiteboard (.office-whiteboard) shows vibe text and a progress bar, but never shows WHAT's being worked on. Add a tiny 2-3 line task preview showing the current top worker's task name, truncated. Cycle through active workers every 8s. WHY: the whiteboard is the natural place to answer 'what's the team working on?' at a glance without switching to the dashboard tab. It's currently wasted space.

ideas.title: Worker Completion Sound Effect
ideas.description: When a worker completes (confetti fires, checkmark appears), play a subtle chime using the Web Audio API — no audio files needed, just a synthesized 3-note ascending tone. Gate it behind a mute toggle in the header. WHY: people leave the tab open in the background. An audio cue when work finishes is genuinely useful — it's the difference between checking back manually and being notified. Every CI/CD tool has this.

ideas.title: Desk Lamp Color Personality
ideas.description: All active desk lamps glow the same warm yellow. Tint each worker's desk lamp to their role-color (--role-color CSS variable is already set per worker). Maya's lamp glows pink-ish, Carlos's glows blue-ish, etc. The lamp becomes a signature. WHY: it's a one-line CSS change (use color-mix with --role-color) that adds surprising personality. When you look at the office, you can tell who's working by lamp color alone.

ideas.title: Daily Office Newspaper
ideas.description: Generate a tiny 'Bloberto Times' newspaper overlay accessible from the whiteboard. Auto-generated daily from activity.json: 'BREAKING: Carlos deployed 3 fixes before lunch', 'OPINION: Is Dave's uptime streak sustainable?', 'WEATHER: Vibe forecast — crushing with a chance of on-fire'. Pure satire. WHY: this transforms raw activity data into narrative. People don't remember stats, they remember stories. Plus it's hilarious.
